section_title: When a contract contains reciprocal promises, and one party to the
content:
53. When a contract contains reciprocal promises, and one party to the
party
contract prevents the other from performing his promise, the contract
preventing
event on becomes voidable at the option of the party so prevented; and he is entitled
which the
to compensation from the other party for any loss which he may sustain in
contract is
to take consequence of the non-performance of the contract.
effect
Illustration
A and B contract that B shall execute certain work for A for a thousand Taka.
B is ready and willing to execute the work accordingly, but A prevents him
from doing so. The contract is voidable at the option of B; and, if he elects to
rescind it, he is entitled to recover from A compensation for any loss which he
has incurred by its non-performance.
Effect of
